title: Baked Ricotta with Figs
description: My Thermomix take on an Ottolenghi-style baked ricotta with honeyed figs — creamy, savoury-sweet, for sharing.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Yotam Ottolenghi and the comforting appeal of baked ricotta with figs. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
steps: Grate the parmesan 8 sec / speed 8. Add ricotta, eggs, lemon zest, salt and pepper. Mix 20 sec / speed 4.
Spoon into an oiled dish and bake at 180°C for 25 minutes until just set. (The Thermomix makes the mix; baking is in your oven.)
Top with the figs, drizzle with honey and scatter thyme. Serve warm with bread.
tips: Drain wet ricotta first so it sets firm.
Lovely with roasted grapes instead of figs.

faq.a: It sits in between — gorgeous as a starter with bread, or as a light dessert.

faq.q: Is this the original Yotam Ottolenghi recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Yotam Ottolenghi and baked ricotta with figs; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
